title: Western blot - alpha-synuclein
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"84i6u","text":"This protocol describes how to detect alpha-synuclein in protein derived from STC-1 cells by western blot using DAB/peroxidase or ECL to visualise the bands.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"55hb7","text":"RIPA buffer (50 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0, with 150 mM sodium chloride, 1.0% Igepal CA-630 (NP-40), 0.5% sodium deoxycholate, and 0.1% sodium dodecyl sulphate)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7m8nr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eq6ld","text":"Protease inhibitors (0.8 μM Aprotinin, 40 μM Bestatin, 140 μM E-64 at, Leupeptin at 20 μM and Pepstatin A at 15 μM, 1mM phenylmethanesulfonyl fluoride)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fi76g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"485kp","text":"Towbin transfer buffer: 25 mM Tris, 192 mM glycine, pH 8.3, with 20% methanol (vol/vol).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"i31c","text":"To prepare 1 L of buffer:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"85v5v","text":"- 800 mL distilled H2O","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1vl5p","text":"- 200 mL methanol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5o0lr","text":"- 3.03 g Tris base","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ftpnt","text":"- 14.4 g Glycine","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"152gj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3beoq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Sample preparation using either protein pellet from an extraction (step 1.1) or direct lysis (step 1.2)

Separate samples (~ 10 ug total protein) by polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis using precast Bolt™ or NuPAGE™ 12%, Bis-Tris, 1.0 mm, Mini Protein Gels at 180 V 00:45:00 or until dye front reaches the bottom of the gel. Run with pre-stained or biotinylated size markers
Wet PVDF membrane with methanol and then equilibrate in Towbin buffer (see materials)
Mix samples with 4x NuPAGE™ LDS sample buffer containing 50 mM dithiothreitol Vortex 00:00:20 twice, then orbital shaker 260 rpm, °C, 00:15:00
Dissolve protein pellets from the TRI Reagent® extraction in 2% SDS containing 8 M urea. Vortex 00:00:30 twice, then orbital shaker 260 rpm, °C, 00:30:00
Dissolve cells in RIPA buffer containing protease inhibitors (see Materials) On ice 00:30:00
Fix proteins to membrane for 00:20:00 with 4% paraformaldehyde
Wash with PBST (PBS containing 0.01% (v/v) Tween™-20) 4 x 00:05:00
Block non-specific binding sites with block solution (PBST containing 2% (w/v) BSA and 0.005 % (w/v) thiomersal) for 01:00:00
Incubate membranes in block solution containing rabbit monoclonal antibody against α-synuclein (1:1250) (ab212184) and β-actin (1:5000) (ab241153) 20:00:00 Room temperature
Wash with PBST 4 x 00:05:00
Incubate membrane with secondary antibody in block solution (step 15.1 or step 15.2)
Wash with PBST 4 x 00:05:00
incubated with peroxidase conjugated streptavidin (1:1000) (Roche) 00:45:00
Wash with PBST 4 x 00:05:00
Wash with PBS 2 x 00:05:00
Visualise bands with DAB if biotinylated secondary and streptavidin-peroxidase were used (step 20.1) or use ECL plus (Amersham) if anti-rabbit peroxidase secondary was used (step 20.4)
Visualize ECL or digitize DAB stained membrane using a ChemiDoc™ MP imaging system (Biorad)
For DAB detection incubate in PBS containing 0.05 % (w/v) 3,3′-diaminobenzidine, 0.015 % (v/v) H 2 O 2 and 0.05 % (w/v) nickel ammonium sulphate for ≤ 00:00:30
Dry membrane 60ºC 1 hour
Incubate membrane with biotinylated goat anti-rabbit IgG (1:1000) (Stratech) 01:15:00 and go to step 16
OR incubate membrane with goat anti-rabbit conjugated to peroxidase (1:25,000) (Stratech) 01:15:00 and go to step 18
Rinse in running tap water > 00:05:00
ECL reagents can be washed off the membrane and the membrane then processed for DAB staining if desired
For chemiluminescent detection use an Amersham ECL plus kit according to the manufacturer's instructions
Place cassette in transfer tank and transfer protein onto 0.2 mm PVDF (polyvinylidene difluoride) membranes (30 V, 01:00:00 in Towbin buffer
Heat samples 70 °C 00:10:00

Soak transfer sandwich components (4 sheets of filter paper and 5 blotting pads) in Towbin transfer buffer and assemble in the transfer cassette in the following order: Starting with the cathode plate 2 x blotting pads 2 x filter paper gel PVDF 2 x filter paper 3 x pad (use 3 because there is less chance of the gel slipping) Use a roller to remove any air bubbles
Centrifuge samples 00:00:30 to collect condensation from tube lid. Vortex 00:00:20 then centrifuge again 00:01:00
Rinse membranes with PBS 00:00:30
creator: Michael J Hurley
